rationale: Israeli strikes on Beirut continue and Trump's comments put the Iran deal at risk — both affect energy price outlook indirectly for Poland, but remain continuation of known dynamics. Score adjusted down: ongoing situation without qualitative change.

top_events.title: Israel expands military control in Gaza, Lebanon and Syria by 1,000sq km; Beirut strikes continue
top_events.summary: Ongoing Israeli military expansion and Beirut strikes continue to add volatility to Middle East energy routes, with indirect effect on Polish energy prices. No qualitative escalation beyond known baseline.

top_events.title: Trump says Israeli attacks on Beirut unjustified, puts Iran deal at risk
top_events.summary: Trump's rhetoric signals friction in US-Iran negotiations, which could keep oil prices elevated — a mild indirect concern for Polish energy costs. This is rhetoric only, no confirmed policy change.

top_events.title: UK and Japan agree £18bn investment deal
top_events.summary: A major bilateral trade deal between UK and Japan has limited direct relevance for Poland, though it signals continued Western economic cooperation in the Indo-Pacific context.
